description: Source URLs for every claim in hgnc_croissant.json. Generated 2026-03-10.

claims.note: The resource is referred to as HGNC throughout the site; RRID is RRID:SCR_002827 for 'HGNC'.

claims.value: The HGNC is a resource for approved human gene nomenclature containing ~42000 gene symbols and names and 1300+ gene families and sets
claims.note: Direct quote from <meta name=Description> tag in homepage HTML.
Direct quote from about page: 'All approved symbols are stored in the HGNC database, www.genenames.org'.
Direct quote: 'We are also registered with the Resource Identification Portal RRID:SCR_002827'.
Derived from lastModified field in REST API /info endpoint: 2026-03-06T14:08:07.425Z. No explicit version string published on the site.
From lastModified field in REST API /info endpoint: 2026-03-06T14:08:07.425Z.
Direct quote: 'all data is released under the Creative Commons Public Domain (CC0) License'.

claims.value: No restrictions are imposed on access to, or use of, the data provided by the HGNC...
claims.note: Verbatim data release policy from About page, section 'Data release policy'.
Direct quote: 'It is a condition of our funding from NIH that the nomenclature and information we provide is freely available to all.'

claims.value: Seal RL, Braschi B, Gray K, McClay J, Tweedie S, Bruford EA. Genenames.org: the HGNC and PGNC resources in 2026. Nucleic Acids Res. 2025 Nov 25:gkaf1229. DOI: 10.1093/nar/gkaf1229. PMID: 41287213
claims.note: The FAQ page (src_faq) lists this as 'Authors are requested to cite' but contains a typographic error: DOI 10.1093/nar/gkaf1329 (which belongs to the RNAcentral 2026 paper, PMID: 41404707). The publications page (src_publications) lists the correct DOI 10.1093/nar/gkaf1229 paired with PMID: 41287213. The citation value in the Croissant file uses the publications-page version with the correct DOI.
Full committee name from About page title and body text.
Direct quote: 'in November 2023, the team moved to the Addenbrooke's campus in Cambridge' and 'the HGNC became a joint project between the University and the EBI'.
